description:
## Background

Settings is currently an application-local destination that hides the Issue Explorer subtree. The accepted navigation model makes Settings a Wouter route but deliberately excludes it from Issue Navigation Entries so transient configuration work does not pollute Issue browsing history.

Triage: `ready-for-agent`.

## What to build

Make Settings available at `/settings` using replacement semantics. Opening and closing Settings preserves and restores the exact underlying Workspace, Issue List View, Issue Search, and selected Issue. Back while Settings is open traverses Issue Navigation history rather than treating Settings as a durable destination.

## Technical Approach

- Add a Settings Route to the top-level Wouter route tree.
- Before entering Settings, retain the current complete Issue Navigation Entry as the underlying destination.
- Enter `/settings` by replacing the current URL rather than pushing a new Issue Navigation Entry.
- Close Settings by replacing `/settings` with the retained underlying Issue route and history state.
- Integrate Back/Forward commands so invoking Back while Settings is visible first restores the underlying Issue destination and then traverses the requested Issue Navigation direction without making Settings an entry.
- Keep the existing Settings draft, save, reset, retry, accessibility, and Workspace sidebar behavior intact.
- Set the Settings window title while routed there and restore the destination title on exit.
- Expose a coordinator operation that later external deep-link delivery can use to close Settings and recover the underlying Issue destination before pushing a link target.

## Considerations

- Settings replacement must not overwrite the semantic Issue Navigation Entry’s index or lose the destination required for restoration.
- Workspace events may arrive while Settings is visible. The hidden Issue Explorer and retained navigation destination must remain consistent with the existing atomic transition gate.
- Direct startup at `/settings` has no prior Issue destination; use the initial default Issue List destination as a conservative underlying state.
- Do not implement external deep-link handling in this ticket.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Settings renders at `/settings`.
- [ ] Opening Settings does not increase Issue Navigation history.
- [ ] Closing Settings restores the exact prior Workspace, view, search, and optional selected Issue.
- [ ] Back while Settings is visible traverses Issue Navigation history and does not leave Settings as a destination.
- [ ] Repeated Settings open/close cycles do not duplicate or corrupt navigation indexes.
- [ ] Window title is `Settings · Beadsmithy` while Settings is open and restores correctly afterward.
- [ ] Existing Settings persistence and Workspace-transition behavior remains green.
- [ ] A reusable coordinator operation can restore the underlying Issue destination before later deep-link navigation.

## Test Plan

- Extend existing Settings integration tests with URL, history index, exact destination restoration, Back behavior, repeated cycles, and titles.
- Exercise Workspace transition events while Settings is visible to ensure snapshot atomicity remains intact.
- Run renderer tests, TypeScript build, Ultracite, and the existing real-desktop Settings acceptance suite.
title: Make Settings a transient route
